response.justification: Bogdan has genuine geospatial domain exposure through GISCollective and Green Map, and distributed systems experience at scale from Dunnhumby, but the role's core requirement is production-grade C++ or Rust for a routing data pipeline. His C++ usage is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a university thesis project — both over a decade ago and far below the seniority and volume expected. His career has moved firmly toward TypeScript/Node.js, with no Rust and no experience in routing tiles, navigation guidance systems, or traffic data integration. The GIS work, while relevant in domain, is at an NGO/open-source application layer rather than the infrastructure and latency-sensitive pipeline level this role demands.
strengths: Geospatial/GIS domain familiarity through GISCollective (event-driven geo-data platform) and Green Map (offline-capable web GIS application)
Distributed systems experience at meaningful scale — low-latency microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Dunnhumby using async I/O and high-performance databases
Strong software craftsmanship culture (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) and demonstrated ability to work across multiple languages and paradigms
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and over 10 years stale (NI LabWindows components in 2014–2015, a university thesis); no Rust experience mentioned anywhere
No experience with routing tiles, navigation pipelines, traffic signal integration, or any production navigation/guidance system
Currently in a career gap and primary trajectory for the last 8+ years has been TypeScript/Node.js web and backend work, not systems-level C++ or geospatial data infrastructure
